title: connector CLI discovers *.preview.tsx files in target repo
description: Implement the first stage of the `packages/connector` CLI: recursively scan a target repository for `*.preview.tsx` files and load their `PreviewConfig` default export.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI command (e.g. `systembook-connector discover [--root <dir>]`) walks the given root directory (default cwd) and finds all files matching `*.preview.tsx`, excluding `node_modules`
For each discovered file, the CLI dynamically imports/transpiles it enough to read its default export and validates it against the `PreviewConfig` shape from `packages/schema`

A file whose default export doesn't match `PreviewConfig` produces a clear, actionable error identifying the file and the validation failure, without crashing the whole discovery run for other valid files
Discovery output is a list of `{ filePath, config: PreviewConfig }` consumable by the next stage (TASK-40)
Running discovery against a sample repo fixture with 2 valid and 1 intentionally malformed `*.preview.tsx` file reports 2 successes and 1 clearly-described failure

steps.details: In `packages/connector/src/cli.ts`, use a lightweight CLI arg parser (e.g. `commander` or manual `process.argv` parsing) to expose a `discover` command accepting `--root`.
Use a glob utility (e.g. `fast-glob`) to find `**/*.preview.tsx` under `--root`, excluding `**/node_modules/**`.

steps.description: Load and validate each file's default export
steps.details: For each matched file, use `esbuild`'s transform/build API (already needed for TASK-41's Vite pipeline) to transpile and evaluate the module in a sandboxed context, extracting the default export. Validate it against a zod schema mirroring `PreviewConfig`.
Aggregate `{ filePath, config }` for valid files and `{ filePath, error }` for invalid ones. Print a human-readable summary (counts and per-file errors) to stdout/stderr.

Create a small fixture directory with 2 valid `*.preview.tsx` files and 1 malformed one (e.g. missing `variants`), run discovery against it, and confirm the reported counts and error message match expectations.

technicalNotes: Evaluating arbitrary TSX files from a target repo requires care — use a proper bundler/transpiler (esbuild) rather than naive `eval`/`require`, since these files may use JSX and TypeScript syntax not natively runnable by Node.
This discovery step only reads `PreviewConfig` metadata; it does not yet build the component itself for preview — that's TASK-40/41.
